raw_consumer_statement: I called to dispute a fraudulent charge after receiving an alert via email that my card was charged an amount that we did not remember making. The email stated that we " tipped '' a merchant named " across the map '' over {$200.00}! I told them the situation which was that my mom and I went to a festival in XXXX, we did purchase tickets to the place but we certainly DID NOT tip {$240.00} and we didn't use it later in XXXX. The Citi bank instead of sending me an alert via email ( which is in my settings ), sends me a letter, which I received XXXX months later stating that because my chip on the card was present at the time of of the purchase of {$240.00} I will be responsible for this charge! I still don't have an explanation as to why they denied the second claim. I called to ask them for documentation so that I can make a formal statement that I am not ok with this decision. After being transferred by very rude representatives multiple times to departments that don't work, they finally connected me to a supervisor who refused to do anything!
